skill.name: technical-clarity
skill.description: Reviews technical writing for learner comprehension, accessibility, jargon density, context gaps, and audience-appropriate clarity.
skill.summary: Audit technical documentation for clear, accessible learner comprehension.
skill.author: 92Bilal26

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell execution, weak cryptography, and system reconnaissance patterns. Review found these are false positives from Markdown fences, inline code formatting, HTTPS examples, accessibility prose, and readability metrics in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection attempts, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown code fences and inline code examples used to teach documentation clarity. They do not execute commands, invoke a shell, or define runnable skill code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown examples or inline formatting. There is no runtime file, command invocation API, or user-controlled shell input.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings occur in descriptive educational text, such as HTTPS guidance, accessibility requirements, and integration labels. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing function, or encryption implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching lines are natural-language documentation guidance. I found no code that selects or uses a weak cryptographic primitive.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern in Review Guidance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance findings are ordinary writing-review instructions and readability metrics. They do not collect host, process, network, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged terms appear in static prose and section headings. No evidence was found for filesystem probing, environment inspection, or host reconnaissance.

content.value_statement: Technical content can feel clear to experts while confusing learners. This skill reviews drafts for audience fit, jargon, missing context, accessibility, and practical revision steps.
actual_capabilities: Reviews documentation, tutorials, examples, and educational content for learner comprehension.
Checks audience level, prerequisite assumptions, reading difficulty, and sentence complexity.
Identifies gatekeeping language, undefined jargon, missing context, and abstract-first explanations.
Suggests clearer definitions, examples, prerequisites, motivations, and accessibility improvements.
Produces structured clarity reports with issue severity, impact, and specific fixes.
Applies accessibility checks for alt text, semantic structure, code readability, and color dependence.
limitations: It provides editorial review and does not verify technical runtime correctness.
Readability scores are guidance and may need external measurement for strict compliance.
It depends on the user to provide the target audience and source material.
It does not replace a review by real learners or accessibility specialists.

use_cases.title: Review a Beginner Tutorial
use_cases.description: Check whether a tutorial explains prerequisites, defines terms, and avoids expert assumptions.

use_cases.title: Prepare API Documentation
use_cases.description: Improve reference text so readers understand purpose, constraints, examples, and error cases.

use_cases.title: Audit Course Material
use_cases.description: Assess lessons for reading level, jargon density, accessibility, and learner confidence.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Clarity Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a short draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this technical explanation for clarity. Identify confusing phrases, undefined terms, and missing context. Target audience: beginner. Draft: [paste text].

prompt_templates.title: Audience-Level Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Match documentation to a defined learner level
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this content for a B1 intermediate learner. Check sentence length, jargon density, prerequisites, examples, and accessibility. Provide prioritized fixes. Content: [paste text].

prompt_templates.title: Tutorial Revision Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn a rough tutorial into a clearer learning path
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this tutorial and create a revision plan. Focus on show-before-tell structure, definitions, motivation, error cases, and learner confidence. Tutorial: [paste text].

prompt_templates.title: Full Documentation Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a full technical clarity audit for this documentation. Include audience assumptions, readability risks, accessibility issues, issue severity, impact, and rewritten examples where useful. Documentation: [paste text].

output_examples.input: A paragraph that says a concept is obvious and uses several undefined terms.
output: Flags gatekeeping language that may make learners feel excluded.
Lists the undefined terms and recommends first-use definitions.
Suggests a clearer opening that explains why the concept matters.

output_examples.input: A tutorial section that starts with abstract theory before showing an example.
output: Identifies the abstract-first structure as a comprehension risk.
Recommends placing a concrete example before the explanation.
Adds prerequisite and motivation notes for the target learner.

output_examples.input: Documentation that relies on color and screenshots to explain errors.
output: Flags visual-only communication as an accessibility issue.
Recommends text labels, captions, and alt text.
Suggests wording that works for screen reader users.

best_practices: Provide the target audience, learning context, and expected prerequisite knowledge.
Ask for issue severity, learner impact, and concrete rewrite suggestions.
Review the revised text again after applying major clarity changes.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic proofreading when you need learner-comprehension analysis.
Do not omit the target proficiency level or expected reader background.
Do not accept readability scores without checking examples, context, and accessibility.

faq.question: What kind of content can this skill review?
faq.answer: It can review technical documentation, tutorials, course lessons, API guides, examples, and educational explanations.

faq.question: Does it rewrite the documentation?
faq.answer: It can suggest rewrites and clearer examples, but its main purpose is a structured clarity audit.

faq.question: Can it check accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks for visual-only cues, missing alt text, code readability, and unclear structure.

faq.question: Does it validate code correctness?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on comprehension and accessibility, not runtime behavior or technical verification.

faq.question: What should I include in my prompt?
faq.answer: Include the draft, target audience, proficiency level, content type, and any known prerequisites.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
